steps.title: Use vertex form with the maximum point
steps.content:
The object reaches maximum height 1,600 feet at t = 10 seconds. This is the vertex of the parabola. We can write the quadratic directly in vertex form without expanding:
$$f(t) = a(t - 10)^2 + 1600$$
where $a$ is the leading coefficient (unknown for now).

steps.title: Find the coefficient a from the initial condition
steps.content:
The object was launched from height 0, so $f(0) = 0$. Substitute into the equation:
$$0 = a(0 - 10)^2 + 1600$$
$$0 = 100a + 1600$$
$$a = -16$$
The parabola opens downward (as expected for an object under gravity).

steps.title: Evaluate the height at t = 15 seconds
